fact: January 9 is exactly 9 days into the year, which means you've already survived 1.3% of the entire year!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when many squirrels start getting super cranky because their buried acorn stashes are running low from winter.
The sun rises about 1 minute later each day around January 9 in most places, but sunsets are getting 2 minutes later - so days are actually getting longer!
January 9 falls smack in the middle of Capricorn season, when people born under this zodiac sign are supposedly extra stubborn (like mountain goats climbing cliffs).
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, January 9 is peak summer - perfect for finding cicadas that sound like tiny chainsaws in the trees.
This date is exactly 19 days after the winter solstice, which means Earth is slowly tilting back toward longer days in the north.
Many evergreen trees are secretly growing new root hairs underground right now, even though everything looks frozen and dead above ground.
January 9 is when polar bears in the Arctic are typically teaching their cubs to hunt seals through breathing holes in the ice - like a deadly game of whack-a-mole.
The moon phase on January 9 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it appears extra bright because winter air is clearer and less humid.
In cold regions, January 9 is prime time for finding animal tracks in fresh snow - like a natural detective story written across your backyard.
Desert plants like cacti are in their deepest sleep mode around January 9, barely using any water and growing slower than a sloth in molasses.
January 9 is exactly 356 days away from the next January 9 - unless it's a leap year, then it's 357 days of waiting!
